response.reasoning: Candidate has 15+ years of software development experience, far exceeding the 6+ year requirement. Strong full-stack background with TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB demonstrated at Optilyz and re:cinq. Current role at re:cinq explicitly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, matching the AI agent development requirement. Has Docker, Kubernetes, AWS CDK experience. Proficient in multiple languages (TypeScript, Java, C++, DLang, Python implied). Strong testing culture (TDD, created Fluent-Asserts library, Trial test runner, Selenium courses). No explicit Nest.js or Vitest mention, but Jest/testing framework experience is evident. Kotlin not mentioned but Java is. Education claim (MIT BS 2009-2012) is unusual given overlapping employment in Romania during the same period — slight credibility concern but not disqualifying given proven hands-on track record. Communication skills evidenced by workshops, talks, and teaching.
strengths: 15+ years full-stack experience with TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB matching the primary stack
Direct AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at current role aligns with required AI agent development skill
Strong testing/TDD background (built test libraries, ran Selenium courses, practiced trunk-based development)
concerns: No explicit Nest.js or Vitest experience listed in the primary stack
Kotlin not mentioned (only Java) for the secondary backend services
Overlapping MIT degree dates with concurrent employment in Romania raises a credibility flag worth verifying
response.justification: Candidate substantially exceeds the experience bar and matches the core stack (TypeScript, Node, React, MongoDB, Docker, cloud) with directly relevant AI-agent infrastructure work at the current role. Gaps around Nest.js, Vitest, and Kotlin are framework-level and easily bridged given the breadth of demonstrated polyglot ability and testing discipline. Worth an interview to validate the secondary stack and the education timeline.
